SayFootnote-1, “It was revealed to me that a band of unseen beingsFootnote-2listened in and said, ‘Indeed, we have heard a wondrous RecitalFootnote-3
that guides to prudence, so we have come to believe in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord.
And thatFootnote-4 most high is the Grandeur of our Lord—He never took a female companion nor a child.
And thatFootnote-5our fool used to speak untruthfully about Allah.
And thatFootnote-6 we thought that humans and unseen beings would never sayFootnote-7 a lie aboutAllah.
And thatFootnote-8certain men among humans used to seek refuge in certain men among the unseen beings, but they only increased them in confusion.
And thatFootnote-9they thought, as youFootnote-10thought, that Allah would never resurrect anyone.
And thatFootnote-11we sought to reach the heaven and found it filled with stern guards and meteors.
And thatFootnote-12we used to take up positions to listen in, but whoever listens now finds a meteor in wait for him.
And thatFootnote-13we do not know whether ill has been desired for those on earth, or if their Lord has desired prudence for them.
And thatFootnote-14some of us are righteous, but some of us are less than that: we have been following divergent roads.
And thatFootnote-15 we realized that we cannot thwart Allah on earth, nor can we thwart Him by fleeing.
And thatFootnote-16when we heard the guidance we believed in it, for whoever believes in his Lord fears neither loss nor confusion.
And thatFootnote-17among us are MuslimsFootnote-18and among us are those who swerve from justice, so whoever has become Muslim—it is they who have pursued prudence.
But as for those who swerve from justice—they have become firewood for Hell.’”
But had they gone straight on the right mode(of life) , We would have given them plenty of water to drink,
so as to test them with it. And whoever disregards the remembrance of his Lord—HeFootnote-19 will insert himinto a mounting punishment.
And(say) that mosques belong to Allah, so do not call upon anyone along with Allah.
AndthatFootnote-20 when the servant of Allah got up, calling upon Him(in prayers) , they almost fell on him in a massFootnote-21 .
SayFootnote-22 , “Indeed, I pray only to my Lord, and I never associate anyone with Him.”
SayFootnote-23, “Indeed, I possess for youFootnote-24neither harm nor prudence.”
SayFootnote-25, “Indeed, no one can protect me from Allah, and I will not find any refuge except with Him.
(I have nothing) except for a proclamation from Allah and His messages, and whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger—for him is the fire of Hell, abiding therein forever.
Only when they see what they are promised, will they then know who has weaker helpers and is fewer in numbers.
SayFootnote-26, “I do not know whether what youFootnote-27are promised is near, or whether my Lord will extend it for a period.
The Knower of the hidden realm—so He does not disclose His hidden realm to anyone
except to a Messenger of His choosing, for He then dispatches guards before him and behind him,
that He may knowFootnote-28 that they have compiled and proclaimed the messages of their Lord. But He has encompassed what they have, and has tallied everything by number.”
